     The Numbers Business Report

Scary Stories to Tell in the Dark (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $68,947,075Details
International Box Office $35,846,633
Worldwide Box Office $104,793,708
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,108,526
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,710,545
Total $9,819,071
Further financial details...

Synopsis

It’s 1968 in America. Change is blowing in the wind… but seemingly far removed from the unrest in the cities is the small town of Mill Valley where, for generations, the shadow of the Bellows family has loomed large. It is in their mansion on the edge of town that Sarah, a young girl with horrible secrets, turned her tortured life into a series of scary stories, written in a book that has transcended time—stories that have a way of becoming all too real for a group of teenagers who discover Sarah’s terrifying tome.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$20,915,346 (30.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.30 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:65.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$28,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,135 opening theaters/3,135 max. theaters, 6.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $90,022,597

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 9th, 2019 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: August 8th, 2019 (Wide), released as Страшные истории для рассказа в темноте (Russia (CIS))
August 9th, 2019 (Wide), released as Histórias Assustadoras Para Contar no Escuro (Portugal)
August 9th, 2019 (Wide) (Peru)
August 9th, 2019 (Wide) (Taiwan)
August 9th, 2019 (Wide) (Brazil)
Video Release: November 5th, 2019 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for terror/violence, disturbing images, thematic elements, language including racial epithets, and brief sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2585 (Cert #52250), 7/3/2019)
Running Time: 111 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Fiction / Reality Crossover
Plot point: Writing and Writers
Social setting: Halloween
Subgenre: Kids Horror
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Entertainment One, CBS Films, Rolling Hills, Starlight Media, Hivemind, 1212 Entertainment, Double Dare You
Production Countries: United States, Canada
Languages: English

Synopsis

It’s 1968 in America. Change is blowing in the wind… but seemingly far removed from the unrest in the cities is the small town of Mill Valley where, for generations, the shadow of the Bellows family has loomed large. It is in their mansion on the edge of town that Sarah, a young girl with horrible secrets, turned her tortured life into a series of scary stories, written in a book that has transcended time—stories that have a way of becoming all too real for a group of teenagers who discover Sarah’s terrifying tome.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$20,915,346 (30.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.30 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:65.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)

Theater counts:3,135 opening theaters/3,135 max. theaters, 6.4 weeks average run per theater
